feature_name: AEP Builder — Step 1 Setup + Step 2 Test & Refine (v2C Inline AI)
review_scope: Step 1 simplified setup form + Step 2 v2C (Inline AI) — two-panel layout with persistent AI Refine left panel and live chat right panel. Five states: base, chip selected, applying, applied/success, error.
quality_bar_assessment: Close to Stripe-level craft. The v2C design is a meaningful improvement over the prior version. The Quick Actions chips, Recent Changes list, and full state coverage (applying, applied, error) are well-designed. Three blocking gaps remain: placeholder copy in the AI response area, missing publish eligibility guard state, and undefined 'View More' expanded state in Step 1.
strengths: Quick Actions chips (More casual, Less aggressive, Add urgency, More formal, Shorter, More empathetic) eliminate the blank-page problem for refinement and match the mental model of 'I know what kind of change I want'
Inline 👍/👎 per-message feedback correctly positioned at moment of reaction — matches Gemini/ChatGPT/Claude rating patterns
Recent Changes list with timestamps gives the manager a record of what has been tried and closes the 'did my change apply?' loop
Applied/Success state (v2C-4) forms a complete feedback loop: '✦ Regenerated' tag on first new message, 'Changes Applied' chip state, and 'Changes applied — new session started' toast
Error state (v2C-5) is correctly handled — 'Generation failed' with 'Something went wrong. Your changes weren't saved.' and 'Try again →', instruction text preserved
Draft badge surfaced in the chat header grounds the manager in the AEP's current status
Simplified Step 1 form (four required fields + View More) dramatically lowers the barrier to first generation while preserving access to advanced configuration
AEP sender label now uses the AEP's configured name ('IT Support Scam AEP') — no longer 'Attacker', which was the highest-priority prior finding

findings.category: Content accuracy / Recognition rather than recall
findings.location: Left panel — AI response area (Reasoning section and action card)
findings.issue: The AI response after a refinement instruction still reads: 'I've created a comprehensive admin UI for the Dune Security training platform… a full assignment activity tracking system…' The action card reads 'Generate Design Element Version 1.' This placeholder copy is from an unrelated tool and makes the refinement confirmation pattern unreadable.
findings.why_it_matters: If this ships with generic LLM responses that don't confirm what behavioral change was applied, security managers will lose trust in the refinement flow immediately. It also blocks meaningful stakeholder review.
findings.recommended_fix: Replace with AEP-behavior-specific copy. After applying 'Less aggressive': 'Done. I've softened the persona's pushback resistance. It will now back off faster when the employee shows hesitation, rather than escalating. Starting a new test session.' Action card: 'AEP Updated' not 'Generate Design Element.'
principles: Match between system and the real world
Trust is part of the UX

findings.category: Error prevention / Trust and risk communication
findings.issue: Publish AEP button appears active in the base state with no visible publish eligibility guard. The 0-sessions disabled state and 1-session warning acknowledgment are documented in user-flow and edge-cases but are not in the Figma designs.
findings.why_it_matters: Publishing is irreversible. A manager who publishes after 0 test sessions could deploy an unvalidated persona in a live campaign against real employees.
findings.recommended_fix: Add disabled state frame (greyed button + tooltip: 'Complete at least one test session before publishing'). Add 1-session warning acknowledgment modal. Consider session counter in step header ('1 session completed').

findings.issue: The 'Reasoning ›' section is collapsed by default (correct) but its content model when expanded is undefined beyond the wrong placeholder. Not defined as permanent feature vs. debug aid.
findings.why_it_matters: Shipping without a defined content model means either placeholder copy or generic LLM reasoning that adds cognitive load without decision-support value.
findings.recommended_fix: Define content model before handoff. If retained: collapsed by default, behavior-specific plain-language ('I removed the dollar amount from the opening message and added a rapport-building phrase before the ask'). If not retained: remove. Do not ship a placeholder.

findings.issue: Step 1 has a 'View More' disclosure with no designed expanded state. If this exposes the advanced six-section fields, the content and label are not visible in any Figma frame.
findings.why_it_matters: Managers doing a first-time setup may not know what 'View More' contains or whether they need it. Advanced fields (compliance frameworks, termination logic) are consequential for AEP behavior.
findings.recommended_fix: Design the expanded state. Label it descriptively ('Advanced Configuration' or 'More scenario detail — optional'). Show which fields are inside so the manager can decide whether they need them.

findings.note: Previous finding DR-01 (2026-05-22): AEP messages were labeled 'Attacker.' Now uses the AEP's configured name ('IT Support Scam AEP'). Employee responder is 'You • Employee.' Resolved.
Previous finding DR-06 (2026-05-22): archetype chips were missing. v2C includes 'Reply as:' with Curious, Skeptical, Hostile, Compliant chips. Resolved.

findings.location: Chat panel — employee sender label consistency
findings.note: Previous finding DR-07 (2026-05-22): inconsistent Employee/John Doe labeling. v2C consistently uses 'You • Employee.' Resolved.

open_questions: [Eng] What does the AI confirm changed after a behavior instruction is applied? Natural-language summary, field diff, or re-statement of updated behavior? Defines Reasoning section content model.
[Both] Is 'Reasoning ›' a permanent customer-facing feature or a debug/transparency aid? If the latter, consider moving to Operator overlay.
[PM] Does 'View More' in Step 1 expose the full six-section form or a curated subset? Which fields are required vs. optional inside?
[PM] Is there a session counter visible in Step 2 so the manager knows how many sessions completed before hitting the Publish guard?
revision_priorities: Replace left panel placeholder copy ('Generate Design Element Version 1') with AEP-behavior-specific confirmation copy — blocking issue for stakeholder review and handoff
Add publish eligibility guard states to Figma — 0-sessions disabled state with tooltip and 1-session warning acknowledgment modal
Design 'View More' expanded state in Step 1 with descriptive label and field inventory
Define and finalize Reasoning section content model — either real behavior-specific copy or remove the section
figma_frames_reviewed: Create New Campaign — Select Templates (Step 1 form)
